summary.intent: Address 5 P2 correctness findings from a Codex review of the signature-pad component in the ars-ui Rust project.
summary.outcome: All 5 findings fixed with TDD (72 native + 5 wasm tests passing), specs synced, and changes committed and pushed after two passing xci-fast CI gate runs.

repo: The project uses a state-machine pattern (Machine/Effect/Props) for UI components; effects are returned from `send()` but must be explicitly `.run()`-ed to fire callbacks — they are not auto-executed.
The CI gate is `cargo xci-fast` and covers clippy, tests, proptest, and wasm browser tests across multiple crates.
Spec files in spec/ are kept in sync with the implementation and are part of the review contract; spec changes are required alongside code changes.
The `@codex review` workflow posts review threads that must be replied to and resolved inline; loop runs until a 👍 verdict is received.
The signature pad's rasterizer lives in `crates/ars-dom/src/signature_raster.rs` while the state machine lives in `crates/ars-components/src/specialized/signature_pad/mod.rs`.
The `11-dom-utilities.md` spec is the authoritative doc for rasterizer behavior including zero-size guards.

code.finding: SyncProps transition must cancel in-flight strokes when the pad becomes disabled/readonly to prevent stuck or committed stale strokes.
on_data_change callback added as a new Props field with a corresponding Effect::DataChange, fired on DrawEnd/Undo/Clear via a data_change_effect() helper.
Hidden input must carry the `disabled` attribute when the pad is disabled so it is excluded from HTML form submission.
SignatureData::is_empty must be point-based (counts recorded points), not stroke-count-based, so data with only empty strokes is treated as blank.
Rasterizer must guard width==0 or height==0 before encoding and return RasterError::Backend instead of producing empty/sentinel bytes (data:,).

workflow: In tests using the state-machine Service, effects returned by `send()` must be explicitly `.run()`-ed to trigger callbacks; not doing so causes callback-assertion tests to silently fail.
Running xci-fast twice (once after implementation, once after spec sync) as a final gate before committing is the project's standard practice.
Codex review is integrated into the PR loop: fix → xci-fast → commit/push → reply+resolve threads → re-trigger → repeat until 👍.

friction: Callback test for on_data_change initially failed because `Service::send` only returns pending effects without running them — required discovering and applying the explicit `.run()` pattern used by sibling tests.
Markdown table alignment in the spec anatomy table caused cosmetic lint noise; the final fix was to restore the original concise cell text rather than re-pad every row.
Multiple sequential reads of the same file were needed because edits changed line numbers, requiring re-location of anchor points between edits.
open_items: Re-trigger `@codex review` after the push and loop until a 👍 verdict is received — the transcript ends before confirming Codex's response to the fixes.
The ScheduleWakeup fallback timer was set but its outcome is not captured in the transcript — verify the CI notification loop is wired correctly for future Codex polling sessions.
